default_policy.journal_style_rule: Use journal-inspired palettes only as non-official style inspiration and always run accessibility review.
default_policy.routing: Scientific figures use palette/; presentations use themes; product UI uses semantic design tokens.

external_sources.name: Matplotlib
external_sources.usage: sampled target colormaps from cloned source

external_sources.name: Material Color Utilities
external_sources.usage: referenced for product/UI dynamic color boundary; no scientific palette copied

external_sources.name: cols4all
external_sources.usage: copied GPL-3 palette colors to palette/external and curated non-core scientific figure candidates; computed c4a_scores for canonical palettes

palettes.name: Okabe-Ito / Color Universal Design
recommended_for: publication figures that must survive color vision differences
avoid_for: more than eight categories without grouping

colorblind_safe.notes: Use redundant encodings for high-stakes figures.

origins.name: Color Universal Design (CUD)
origins.license_note: CUD page by Masataka Okabe and Kei Ito; use with attribution to Color Universal Design principles.

palettes.notes: Values are the standard Okabe-Ito palette used with CUD guidance; the downloaded CUD page is recorded as the verified upstream guidance source.

palettes.name: Nature Publishing Group inspired
recommended_for: manuscript plots needing restrained categorical contrast
avoid_for: accessibility-critical category encoding without grayscale and CVD checks

colorblind_safe.notes: Journal-inspired palettes require colorblind and grayscale review before submission.

origins.name: ggsci
origins.license_note: ggsci license applies; journal-inspired palettes are not official brand specifications.

palettes.disclaimer: Not official journal branding or publisher style guidance; this is a journal-inspired palette sourced from ggsci.

sampling.method: linear interpolation from cloned segmented colormap data
